channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [6] More spin flow with less dissipation
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Prasanta Kumar Muduli, Pranaba Kishor Muduli

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Spin current in ferrimagnetic multilayer, relevant to magnetic materials and spintronics.

Tags: #spintronics #magneticmaterials #transport

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 18 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03267-7 More spin current can be produced with less energy lost at the source, thanks to inter-magnet pumping that rebalances angular momentum dissipation between sublattices in a ferrimagnetic multilayer.
item.dc:title: [6] More spin flow with less dissipation
item.dc:creator: Prasanta Kumar Muduli, Pranaba Kishor Muduli
Pranaba Kishor Muduli
item.description:
Last authors: Prasanta Kumar Muduli, Pranaba Kishor Muduli

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Spin current in ferrimagnetic multilayer, relevant to magnetic materials and spintronics.

Tags: #spintronics #magneticmaterials #transport

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 18 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03267-7 More spin current can be produced with less energy lost at the source, thanks to inter-magnet pumping that rebalances angular momentum dissipation between sublattices in a ferrimagnetic multilayer.

item.title: [7] Controllable hydro-thermoelastic heat transport in ultrathin semiconductors at room temperature
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: F. X. Alvarez, K. J. Tielrooij

Authors: S. Varghese, J. Tur-Prats, J. D. Mehew, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental heat transport in ultrathin 2D semiconductors (MoSe2, MoS2).

Tags: #2Dmaterials #semiconductors #heattransport #vanderWaals

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03297-1 The combination of viscous heat flow and thermoelastic effects leads to a non-diffusive heat transport regime in MoSe2 and MoS2. Moreover, it can be controlled through the variation in sample thickness and by choosing between continuous and pulsed heating.
item.dc:title: [7] Controllable hydro-thermoelastic heat transport in ultrathin semiconductors at room temperature
item.dc:creator: Last authors: F. X. Alvarez, K. J. Tielrooij; Authors: S. Varghese, J. Tur-Prats, J. D. Mehew, et al.
J. Tur-Prats
J. D. Mehew
D. Saleta Reig
R. Farris
J. Camacho
J. A. Haibeh
A. Sokolov
P. Ordejón
S. Huberman
A. Beardo
F. X. Alvarez
K. J. Tielrooij
item.description:
Last authors: F. X. Alvarez, K. J. Tielrooij

Authors: S. Varghese, J. Tur-Prats, J. D. Mehew, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental heat transport in ultrathin 2D semiconductors (MoSe2, MoS2).

Tags: #2Dmaterials #semiconductors #heattransport #vanderWaals

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03297-1 The combination of viscous heat flow and thermoelastic effects leads to a non-diffusive heat transport regime in MoSe2 and MoS2. Moreover, it can be controlled through the variation in sample thickness and by choosing between continuous and pulsed heating.
